mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical relationship with FEDFUNDS changes (Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 9.0 percent, severe labor market slack signals impending corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 9.0 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer demand recovery, which amplifies corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day SPX returns (interaction term coefficient insignificant at p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during mid-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls signal robust economic activity and rising consumer spending power, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows zero predictive power for 5-day SPX direction (Rank IC consistently below 0.01 across all rolling windows).

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index crosses above 220.0, accelerating inflation forces monetary tightening expectations, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 58% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 220.0.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, reducing recession risk premiums and driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction effect between GDPC1 and RSAFS yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in predicting 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and supply bottlenecks, triggering profit margin compression and negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during late-cycle expansions are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns in > 65% of cases.
